source: RMC-BestFit/src/RMC.BestFit/Estimation/MaximumAPosteriori.cs @ fc28c0c -- exact oracle values dumped from the REAL C# MaximumAPosteriori via tools/oracle_emitter (Task T12). NelderMead is a deterministic simplex (no RNG, no parallel reduction), so the optimized posterior-mode quantities (parameter/max_log_likelihood/aic/bic) reproduce to rel 1e-9 across the 4 independently-built cores. Same tolerance tiers as mle_normal_smoke.json: diagonal covariance/standard_error/correlation at rel 1e-5 (numerical-Hessian finite-difference sensitivity), off-diagonal covariance/correlation at an absolute tolerance (near-zero for the Normal posterior; rel would divide by ~0). Note MAP != MLE here: the default Uniform priors' bounds shift the posterior mode (sigma) relative to the pure MLE.
